description:
You are consulting as a security engineer who is rotating a pair of legacy
    credential files.  A junior colleague already placed the credentials in:

        /home/user/credentials/db_creds.txt
        /home/user/credentials/api_key.txt

    1. Create a **gzip-compressed tar archive** named
          /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.tar.gz
       that contains *both* of the files above and **nothing else**.

    2. Immediately verify the archive by listing its content with:
          tar -tzf /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.tar.gz
       and redirect the output into a plaintext log file located at:
          /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.log

    Required log format (the automated test will check for this exact two-line
    sequence, no extra characters or whitespace):

        credentials/db_creds.txt
        credentials/api_key.txt

    Summary of the final filesystem state that will be checked:

    • /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.tar.gz  – exists and is a valid
      gzip-compressed tar archive.

    • /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.log  – exists and contains exactly
      the two lines shown above (newline at the end of each line is acceptable).

    Perform the task entirely from the terminal.  No root privileges are
    required.  Do not move or delete the original credential files; simply
    archive them and generate the verification log.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files before the task starts -->
    Directory: /home/user/credentials          (permissions: 755)
    File: /home/user/credentials/db_creds.txt  (permissions: 600)
          Contents:
              username=db_user
              password=S3cur3P@ssw0rd
    File: /home/user/credentials/api_key.txt   (permissions: 600)
          Contents:
              API_KEY=ABCDEF1234567890

    Directory: /home/user/backup               (permissions: 755) – already created and writable.

    <!-- Expected artefacts after successful task completion -->
    File: /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.tar.gz
          • Must be a gzip-compressed tar archive.
          • The archive must *only* contain exactly two entries with the
            following internal paths (no leading “./”):
                credentials/db_creds.txt
                credentials/api_key.txt

    File: /home/user/backup/creds_backup_20300101.log
          Exact content (including final newline after the second line):
              credentials/db_creds.txt
              credentials/api_key.txt

    Automated tests will:
      1. Verify the archive exists at the exact path and is a valid gzip file.
      2. Extract the file list from the archive and compare it to the two
         required entries (order must match).
      3. Check that the log file exists and its content matches the required
         two-line format precisely.
name: task_000000_93f20797
